# source_class v0

> One axis for what an outcome claim in agent evidence rests on: a typed
> source-class vocabulary, a mapping of several 2026 dialects onto it, ten
> vectors, and a dependency-free checker. A vocabulary, not a standard.

An agent evidence record that says `outcome: blocked` does not say how that was
determined: a gateway refusal before dispatch, a runtime deny branch, and an
external monitor all produce the same value. The `source_class` axis types the
basis of the claim so a reader knows which one it holds. The unit that matters
is the axis and its invariants; the checker is small enough to rewrite from its
docstring.

## Files
- [README.md](README.md): the problem, the axis, why it is load-bearing, the non-claims.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): entry point for coding agents; how to run and edit.
- [map/axis.json](map/axis.json): seven values, five invariants, canonicalization declaration.
- [map/dialects.json](map/dialects.json): the mapping of external vocabularies onto the axis, with credit.
- [vectors/cases.json](vectors/cases.json): records plus the verdict a conforming reader reproduces.
- [check.py](check.py): the reader, standard library only, deterministic.
- [schema/source-class-record.schema.json](schema/source-class-record.schema.json): JSON Schema (2020-12) for a native record.
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md): correcting or authoring a dialect row; correcting the axis.

## Run
`python3 check.py` prints per-case verdicts and the corpus digest. No install, no network.
